_provenance.note: This config file was RECONSTRUCTED after the fact. Phase 017 research was executed as a single-shot `codex exec` delegation that bypassed the sk-deep-research loop driver entirely. The loop never ran, so no real init/iterate/synthesize phases happened. This file approximates what the config would have contained if the proper loop had been used. It is NOT a record of an actual iterated research session. See iterations/iteration-001.md for the full retrofit rationale.

real_execution.command: cat research-prompt.md | codex exec --model gpt-5.4 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" -c service_tier="fast" --full-auto -

topic: Memory refactor vs deprecation — is the current Spec Kit Memory system genuinely valuable, or redundant with spec kit documentation? Investigate alternatives: wiki-style spec updates, handover-only, findings-only, or full deprecation.

reducer.note: Reducer was NOT run — single-shot retrofit. Strategy file machine-owned sections were backfilled from research.md, not generated by the reducer.

retrofit_policy.recommended_on_next_similar_run: Use `/spec_kit:deep-research:auto` instead of `codex exec`. The loop driver produces real state files and proper iteration tracking. See .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/006-canonical-continuity-refactor/prompts/README.md for the enforcement note that prevents this failure mode in phase 018.
